key_insight: A constrained LLM front end can safely automate finite-element setup by handling parsing and geometry generation, while deterministic human-written FEniCS templates keep the solver core reliable.
engineering_relevance: This enables natural-language-driven multi-physics simulation workflows for solid mechanics without letting the model directly write weak forms or numerical solver code.
method: LLM converts prompts into validated JSON specifications.
Deterministic dispatcher maps specs to five fixed FEniCS/UFL templates.
LLM is only used for non-catalog geometry via Gmsh and retry feedback.
evidence_signal: In evaluation, the parser reached 100% final valid parses on 15 prompts, and the custom-geometry path achieved 90% success on 10 cases, while benchmark simulations matched analytic/published results from sub-percent to about 2–5% error.
watch_item: The approach depends on tightly scoped templates and validation, so performance may drop for geometries or physics outside the supported catalog.
paper_title: A Constrained Natural-Language Interface for Variational Multi-Physics Finite Element Simulations in FEniCS
